claim: Gemini 3 demonstrates superior multi-step code generation via voxel art, ray tracing, particle physics simulators in browser
actionable: Use Gemini 3 for complex visual/interactive code generation; test multi-artifact scenarios with high iteration counts

claim: Gemini 3 context window management shows regression vs 2.5: ignores new instructions, rehashes old answers in long conversations
actionable: Start new context threads frequently in Gemini 3; avoid extending single conversation beyond 10-15 exchanges

claim: Nano Banana image generation quality matches Gemini 3 for power-of-10 animations and ray tracing tasks
actionable: Use Nano Banana as cost-effective alternative to Gemini 3 for image generation in animations and simulations

claim: Physics simulations (tokamak, particle collider, gravity) in Gemini 3 are visually plausible but scientifically inaccurate
actionable: Do not rely on LLM-generated physics simulations for actual scientific work; validate with domain experts

claim: Golf swing analysis by Gemini 3 (78/100 score, shoulder/hip rotation assessment) appears superficially useful but lacks expert validation
actionable: Have domain experts validate domain-specific analysis (sports, medicine, law); LLM plausibility does not guarantee accuracy

claim: Image-to-voxel conversion works well for stylized assets but struggles with photorealistic detail preservation
actionable: Use Gemini 3 image-to-voxel for artistic stylization; avoid for photorealistic detail conversion

claim: Gemini 3 coding improvements are most visible in long conversations with multiple artifacts; short one-shot examples misleading
actionable: Evaluate Gemini 3 via multi-turn conversations with iterations, not cherry-picked one-shot demos; expect context degradation

claim: One-shot demo quality does not reflect actual long-form productivity; continuous evaluation more important than showcase examples
actionable: Create multi-turn eval suites; do not rely on polished one-shot examples to validate model quality
